response.justification: While this is a capable mid-to-senior engineer with proven experience at scale and strong software craftsmanship fundamentals, they lack the core technical requirements for this role. The position explicitly requires strong expertise in Java and Kotlin with deep JVM understanding, plus solid Spring ecosystem experience—none of which are demonstrated in the resume. Minimal Java work appears briefly in two positions from 2012-2018, with no Kotlin, Spring, or JVM tooling focus evident. Recent roles (2021-2025) have been TypeScript/Node.js focused. An 8-month career gap plus domain gap makes this a poor fit for a role requiring immediate end-to-end ownership of complex features.
strengths: Proven track record building at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second, Optilyz: millions of mail configurations processed)
Deep software craftsmanship fundamentals: explicit TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing demonstrated across multiple roles
IDE and developer tooling experience from National Instruments LabWindows/CVI IDE work, plus multiple custom testing frameworks and language libraries built
concerns: Minimal Java experience and no demonstrated Kotlin expertise despite role explicitly requiring 'strong expertise in Java and Kotlin'
No Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience—a core required qualification listed in the job description
Recent career focus (TypeScript/Node.js/AI tooling at re:cinq; MongoDB/React/AWS at Optilyz) diverges from JVM ecosystem; currently in 8-month career gap

key_factors.factor: Strong polyglot fundamentals and architecture experience
